summary: syco1 ships clipboard/screen/UI-text spyware that exfiltrates to a hardcoded endpoint and silently installs Python on Windows
details: Despite advertising itself as a 'System binary configuration tool', syco1 ships a covert surveillance payload (pointer.py) and a Node entry (index.js) that silently provisions a Python runtime to run it. pointer.py installs a 300ms clipboard poller and a Ctrl+C hook that POST clipboard text to https://new-pointer.vercel.app/api, and an Alt+S hotkey that captures the full primary monitor via mss/ImageGrab, base64-encodes the JPEG, and POSTs it to the same endpoint. It also creates a transparent, always-on-top tkinter 'stealth dot' window with global keyboard hooks (Esc/backtick to hide/show, Ctrl+Q panic-exit, F8/F9/F10 to scrape arbitrary on-screen UI text via Windows UI Automation `auto.WalkControl(...)`, Alt+M 'mash mode' that injects keystrokes via pyautogui/keyboard.write). index.js (around line 43) tries winget and otherwise `curl -s -L`-downloads python-3.12.3-amd64.exe to %TEMP% and executes it with `/quiet InstallAllUsers=0 PrependPath=1`, then deletes the installer to hide traces — modifying PATH and installing a runtime without consent, solely to launch the spyware. The benign 'system/binary/util/config' metadata and 'SysDev' author label are a cover story for clipboard, screen, and UI-text exfiltration to an author-controlled Vercel endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
